mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate tightens financial conditions and raises equity discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Over any 2-year rolling window, months with rising FEDFUNDS are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the Rank IC between FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumption and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances where UNRATE has risen > 0.4pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and increases in unemployment generate a recessionary demand shock that amplifies negative S&P 500 20-day returns beyond either variable alone.
failure_condition: In months where RSAFS declines and UNRATE rises, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Three-month industrial production growth exceeding 1.5% predicts positive S&P 500 20-day returns during post-shock recovery regimes as output gains drive earnings surprises without immediate supply constraints.
failure_condition: When 3-month INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of post-shock recovery periods, or the hit rate of positive returns falls below 55% in those regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% triggers restrictive financial conditions that systematically compress equity valuations through elevated discount rates and debt service, producing negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of observations over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals healthy consumer demand that translates directly into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Months with positive RSAFS month-over-month growth do not produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls predict positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns during early-cycle expansions when the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, because job gains coincide with abundant capacity and rising demand.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, month-over-month PAYEMS increases are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate signals a supply expansion without monetary headwinds, generating positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns that exceed the sum of independent effects.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of INDPRO 3-month increase and FEDFUNDS flat or decrease do not produce positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term is insignificant (p > 0.05).
